name: FDA approved Miplyffa for NPC neurological symptoms with miglustat
summary: The regulatory signal is meaningful but indication-specific and does not establish general aging benefit.
statement: FDA approved Miplyffa (arimoclomol), in combination with miglustat, for neurological symptoms associated with Niemann-Pick disease type C in adults and children 2 years and older.
population_or_model: Adults and children 2 years and older with Niemann-Pick disease type C
quantitative_note: FDA described a 50-patient 12-month randomized trial and slower disease progression on the rescored 4-domain NPC Clinical Severity Scale among patients using background miglustat.
caveats: Approval is for NPC neurological symptoms with miglustat, not for aging, proteostasis enhancement, or disease prevention in otherwise healthy people.
The label context includes hypersensitivity warnings, including hives and angioedema.
FDA noted common side effects including upper respiratory tract infection, diarrhea, and decreased weight.
